title: T-063 — UI: TEC-095 Traceability Search foundation (cross-tagged → 05-WAREHOUSE)
prompt:
# T-063 — UI: TEC-095 Traceability Search foundation (cross-tagged → 05-WAREHOUSE)

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §9.4, §10A TEC-095
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira
Prototype: `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/technical/other-screens.jsx:823-901` (traceability_screen)

## Goal
Forward/backward lot-genealogy search foundation; full query lives in 05-WAREHOUSE.

Search by item_code or lot. Result list links to /warehouse/lots/[lp_id] for full genealogy.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- app/(app)/technical/traceability/page.tsx [create]
- tests/ui/tec-095-traceability.test.tsx [create]

## Out of scope
- Genealogy DAG (05-WAREHOUSE / 12-REPORTING)

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the page renders, when compared to prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/technical/other-screens.jsx:823-901, then search bar, result list, and link styling match — structural + visual + interaction parity verified by RTL.
2. Given empty query, when on page, then prompt 'Enter item or lot' shows.
3. Given clicking a result row, when fired, then navigation goes to /warehouse/lots/[lp_id].
4. UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.

## Test strategy
- RED tests for parity + navigation.
- Run: pnpm vitest run tests/ui/tec-095-traceability.test.tsx
- For UI parity, capture screenshots/artifacts and Playwright trace/video/artifacts where applicable; attach artifact paths in closeout.

## Risk red lines
- Do not implement full genealogy here.
- Prototype naming red-line: translate any legacy prototype/domain labels `fa`/`FA5100`/Factory Article to canonical `fg`/FG, any `PR-code` wording to WIP/intermediate, and any `process_stage`/`process_code` to `manufacturing_operation_name`; do not introduce legacy field names in final code, labels, tests or schemas.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
- Do not paste prototype JSX verbatim — translate to production shadcn primitives per `_meta/prototype-labels/translation-notes-technical.md`.
- Do not use raw HTML <select> — use shadcn Select.
- Do not use inline styles — use Tailwind classes; conditional via cn()/cva().
- Do not import @radix-ui/* outside packages/ui (ESLint guard).

## Prototype parity
- Canonical anchor: `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/technical/other-screens.jsx:823-901` (traceability_screen) — see `_meta/prototype-labels/prototype-index-technical.json` entry and `_meta/prototype-labels/translation-notes-technical.md` for shadcn translation guidance.
- Evidence policy: `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md` — closeout must include screenshot artifacts + Playwright trace/video where applicable, plus axe results or documented blocker.
